item.title: Friday Dump
item.description:
God bless you?
Whole aisle to himself, yessir
For the supremely lazy
Mmmmmmm
Roughing it
My burlesque name!? Spelled just right!? Thanks Honey!
Bored as fuck
At 3 am, mostly
The two extremes
I'll take either's lifestyle
Bitch ass brain, making me take unaffordable drugs to reach baseline
*Insert Nelson Ha Ha gif here*
Yup
No one likes a dry pussycat
New Rick Roll
Gimme gimme gimme
Oh, Miette
Confusing attention for affection again, huh?
Satisfying crunch
Cuddlemonsters unite!
He he
Feels Egyptian in style
Silly critters
One corner of the room, maybe
We literally fought a World War over this...I will end you on sight
Need room for activities
Good fuckin luck, boy-o
Womp womp
I just hear JFK saying hahd
I don't dress it, but I am in fact very much both
Libra with a Virgo cusp...guess I'm undercooked
Fucking yogurt talking shi...oh, I'm the asshole
The fingertip blood just gets me
Spot on
Because praying ain't shit
It's true!
Megan Mullally, for the win, every time
I can't stop you, Paige
WAP
Cock of the walk
Self care is important
Want some food, or lost your appetite
Grandma gives gains
That's a no from me, dog
Perpetually
Weird
She's so sweet about it
??
Growing into its head
Double wrapped

we did some weird shit.

everywhere
Gnostic Christianity is really wild, according to the Gnostics, Jesus named Simon "Peter" because he was dumb as a rock.
owo

http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging http://bsky.abbii.gay: For Bluesky The internet is fucking weird.

1
2. Work smarter, not harde... oh
3. Normal people: goes to college and learns useful skill so they can find a good job this guy:
4
5. The women in front of her was about to sprint6
6. Musical doggo
7. Squishi horse
8. Gym sisters
9
10. Your proudest unzip
11. Behold... magic
12. Truly inspirational
13. Come on, it's Friday! Gimme a break
14
15. I think a good life advice I just added. P.S. This a screenshot from the game I'm currently working on. If you like wacky adventures and passive aggressive humor, that you might like my Stanley Parable inspired game called Do Not Press The Button. Please wishlist on Steam, as it helps us small inide devs: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Dont_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse_The_Game/
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: I'm numb.
item.description:
My pup, Kairi, had a vet appointment today. They said surgery won't help and that she has less than a month. We're crushed. I've never seen my husband cry like this. It's breaking my heart. Hug your pets.
We love you, sweet girl.
item.dc:creator: Zinfandella

item.title: So high
item.dc:creator: SkyPigeon123

item.title: LMAO, I Wonder Why!
item.description:
Just my hot take of course but this reeks to me of a new chief who's worried about potential budget cuts. 'Cause we all know crime pays - if you're law enforcement or private prisons. And of course with the changes coming next January we're certainly going to need some fresh bodies to replace those 'horrible illegal immigrants' who've been sent home. Full article - https://www.yahoo.com/news/lapd-chief-says-people-arent-202657586.html
I love it when the problem, or at least a bit part of it, is starting you in the face. Can I see a show of hands - who thinks something will actually be done???
This was the icing on the cake for me, there's a true gem in every one of these paragraphs. "...responsible adoption and use of technology with a steadfast commitment to privacy." Use of technology: until body cameras no longer come with an off switch, or there are actual consequences when it's used improperly, I call bullshit. "...concerns that the LAPD under his watch would be overly aggressive." I dunno, repeatedly killing people who called you for help and innocent bystanders and then hiding behind 'Qualified Immunity' will do that. "... a shared commitment to safety and mutual respect.” Please, if this were the case do you really think cops would be so sensitive to being recorded, IN PUBLIC, when performing searches, arrests, etc.? I'm not talking about those dumbasses that are right up on the cops and are an actual and potential threat, I'm talking about those recording from a responsible distance and not interfering in any way. And yet the cops will shut them down lest the public get a true, unfiltered view of how they perform their duties.
item.dc:creator: divjnky

item.title: Yelling into the void
item.description: *haven't worked in a professional environment (I've been spending the last 14 years volunteering as tutor and substitute teacher, for various reasons.) Nervous, excited, nauseous. Not used to dealing with people in academia (job is at a university). Haven't told my family, because I didn't want to have to tell them afterwards that I didn't get it. This could change my life for the better. Really just needed to shout it out.
item.dc:creator: DutchBoeremeisie

item.title: Day 12
item.description: https://youtu.be/CknbyQPhE-4 How quickly it becomes difficult to do it every day. I knew if I didn't walk as soon as I got home, I wouldn't. And it took me a bit to get back around here to post it. But it happened. And will happen tomorrow. And the next day. And at some point I'll break out the cane and see how I do with that, but I'll have my wife around just to try and help keep things safe. I've also got to tackle steps and non-flat surfaces. This is going to take some time. https://ieh.one/t/day-12/102
item.dc:creator: daychilde

item.title: I feel like a big boy now.
item.description: Fan motor bearing was shot. I’m 40 years old. Feels good tho. Edit: seeing the comments fill up with people with similar stories is pretty awesome.

item.title: FTW
item.dc:creator: fulcrims

item.title: I am NOT FAMOUS (but I know a few pretty famous people)
item.description:
I went to New York ComicCon (or NYCC if you're a comic nerd) in October but I'm on deadline, and didn't upload pics to Imgur. If you're interested here are a few moments of my adventure in The Big Apple! This is me with ERICA FETT, who is a super famous cosplayer and pin up model. Love her, she's the absolute best.
This is the entrance to the con area. It was PACKED!
Drinks with MING CHEN (he was on Kevin Smith's TV show 'Comic Book Men' that ran for 7 seasons on AMC, I think it was on after 'The Walking Dead')
Signed some copies of WEIRD TALES at the Blackstone booth. I wrote a short story in it called 'The World Breaker'!
Got invited to a part at ATARI, where I got to play the Power Rangers game that's in development. SO FUN! I'm not even a gamer, but this was amazing.
Had brunch at Lillie's Victorian Establishment, which is the best brunch I've ever had in my life. Holy crapola. If you're in Manhattan, GO THERE!
Best. Bruch. Of. All. Time. Anyway, that was just a quick picture dump. As a Canadian I love getting an excuse to travel to NYC and poke around, see old friends, and of course geek out at ComicCon every October. Have an awesome weekend! (and if you're ever at a comic con, please find me and come say hello!!)
item.dc:creator: BlakeNorthcott

item.dc:creator: Skullwriter

item.title: I hope for the wall
item.description: Colors are correct. tl;dr The past 15 months have been the worst I've experienced in my 40 years, with a new traumatic horror every month that has broken me in every way a life can be broken. This last horror might actually be the end of it all. I hope it is. It started with losing my bulldog to sudden liver disease with the end coming within a week of discovering there was a problem (he skipped 2 meals). I was told I had 6 months with him. Then 3. Then 1. Then "he isn't going to make it through the weekend". The next month my Frenchie puppy got out through a hole in my fence the construction next door created and didn't tell me. She got hit and died instantly. I found her. She was out of the house for maybe 5 minutes before I realized she had gotten out and that's all it took. I was laid off in Jan and am still unemployed with dozens of rejections. In March I was hit on my motorcycle by someone running a red light separating my collar bone and shoulder. Even with more planned extensive surgery it will never be back to what it was. I'll never be able to create some of my art again (blacksmith), I'll never be able to workout like I did (powerlifter) and I'll always be in some form of permanent pain. The driver is 21 with the most minimal insurance coverage with the max payout of 15k with 1/3 to medical and 1/3 to lawyer so there won't be any kind of financial relief to offset. In July my partner ended things. They moved to a new city and the distance wasn't working, it was amicable. I loved them so deeply I wanted them to be happy and supported them in the move because it was the right decision for them and their career. We were talking about the next forward positive step for us when they ended things out of nowhere. They were in a new relationship 48 hours later. I found out last weekend it had been going on for months. There is so much from the minor to the devastating, but October was supposed to be a light at the end of the tunnel. I got a job. They sent me hardware. I was excited, I was hopeful. The night before my start date they rescinded the offer, no reason given why. Then today I get the next horror after a Dr follow-up of scan results. It started with losing 40 pounds in the past 3 months (I struggle to lose 1-3 when I'm actually trying). Then debilitating migraines a few weeks ago. Finally I started getting light headed every time I stand up, worsening until one day I blacked out. Hospital visits and scans later we think we know what might be causing it. A mass in my brain. So everything could finally be over and I just want it to be. I have always trudged through a life that wouldn't stop kicking me, from an abusive childhood to this whole year+, I just moved forward because I didn't have another option. I can't end it myself. The last person in my life I love (as friends) would not be able to handle it. They've had similar trauma and struggle with their own mental health and if I just took the quick exit I don't think they would ever be okay. But this mass could be my way out. It could finally end a life of getting kicked on the floor again and again. A life that has taken everything from me. And it wouldn't be my fault, it wouldn't be my choice, it would just finally be an end to everything. Because the alternative could be that it's not. The alternative could be a future of brain surgery, possible brain damage, chemo, and worse. Or it could be nothing and then I get to live hopeless where I'm just supposed to "keep fighting". I'm in therapy, I have a psych, I am medicated. Even a PTSD diagnosis doesn't matter, it just proves that shit is fucked and it's not anywhere close to being over. I go to the gym regularly to workout whatever I can. I eat properly. I try and find any joy I can every day. I am doing everything I can think of to try and just keep from living in despair every day. I know I can build up a lot of these things again, it wouldn't be the first time. I can find a new love, I can adopt a new dog, I can find a new job. I've done all of that multiple times over my life. I am fighting. I don't want to fight anymore. I just can't. Sometimes there is no light at the end of the tunnel, and the most you can hope for is the brick wall at the end of the line. I hope for the wall. I'm not sharing this with family or friends until I know more, I don't think they are going to have the same reaction I am. But this is place to scream into the void, or in my case welcome it.
item.dc:creator: LordLlama971

item.title: I don't like change. And definitely not when it's worse

item.description: P.s. thanks for all the laughs
item.dc:creator: GregoryLies

item.title: A decision has been made.
item.dc:creator: candiedyms

item.title: Wollard is celebrating his 24th birthday today....
item.description: Wollard came to us a starving, emaciated, body so covered with fleas that we didn't realize his coat was white, and covered with motor oil. As a baby he was found in a bucket of motor pil, exhausted from trying not to drown. The person who previously had him tried to kill him by tossing him in the bucket of oil. A neighbor found out what had been done and fetched him out of the bucket and immediately brought him to us. We bathed him and warmed him up and fed him three whole bottles of formula and promised him he would never b abused again. He was our first rescues and has been a major part of our family for the past 24 years. We know he doesn't have much time left and is showing obvious signs of last days with us. We don't want to acknowledge that he will soon b gone because it's literally gonna break our hearts. We have appreciated every moment he has been with us and love him more each day. He is a true survivor.
item.dc:creator: Tonkie1

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1184 (wendygo's)
item.description:
I have made coffee for the first time in days. This is an improvement. You have to keep going. We have to keep going. Let's get this bread.
I miss this man. And as he said elsewhere, most of magic is simply knowing something other people don't.
Except for Pippin. Pippin, if I let him, won't wake up until 7 or even 8! He ends up often waking up when I wake up these days.
I still have these days.
Man. This is why you need to get forklift certified bruh.
We all know now that Evangelicals don't actually respect and honor shit except money, themselves, and frumpy antichrists.
Be human, do crime.
I've realized that one of the reasons I'm so down is that I have nothing to look forward to. Well, except for my Scythian bow I ordered, but I have no idea when it's coming, so that's not exactly concrete.
I learned today in the book I'm reading that a piece of billion plus years old oceanic crust is exposed somewhere near Payson, AZ. Which is only like an hour and a half away.
I've decided I need to see this fragment of a long-lost ocean for myself. It's a geologic feature known as an ophalite; a section of Earth's oceanic crust and the underlying upper mantle that has been uplifted and exposed, and often emplaced onto continental crustal rocks and often record ancient collisions between continents and cratons.
Apparently, this chunk is pretty well preserved, covering a decent area, though I never knew the thing existed until today. This slab of old seabed is old as fuck, dating back to the Statherian eon of the Precambrian, back 1.73 BILLION years. I want to touch it. I want to smoke a cigar on this ancient ocean bottom. I want to do it before Christmas, weather permitting.
I also decided I need a more long term trip goal, so I'm going to aim for making a pilgrimage to visit Pando in Utah. Hopefully I can convince some friends to join me there. It would, incidentally, be cool to take a trip on my trip and do some mushrooms inside what is probably the oldest living organism in the world. (Pando is a clonal Quaking Aspen Grove that might be as old as 81,000 years, and also happens to be the world's largest tree.)
I suppose if any imgurians are down to camp in the woods you could meet me there, but I'm probably not going to take Pippin on the trip. I will bring some homemade mead. Oooh, I should make a special mead for this and call it Pando-monium. Not sure what ingredients I'd use though.
So anyway. Gotta keep going so I can do the things.
If nothing is possible, nothing is permitted.
Aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
Mood
Alien landscape by Wayne Barlow
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "Breeeep? Mmmrrr. I'm sorry. [Unintelligible muttering]"
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Dammit man!
item.description: Leave some P*ssy for the rest of us! EDIT: Sadly I don't know the sauce, but still thanks for the upvotes, Imgurians! Hope you have a nice weekend! I do some reviews (As a hobby, not paid!) at https://hypeimmune.wordpress.com/ feel free to check them!
item.dc:creator: Skullwriter
